fact: Her ritual involves feathers, gourds, and machetes to symbolize her swift and protective nature.
Ceremonies held in sacred groves or near ceiba trees.
Call upon her energies to aid in transformation and to dispel negativity.

abilities: Controlling and sustaining the life-giving forces of nature.
fact: Any invocation with no seeress or priestess (hlmt, 'Dreamer') present in her sanctuary is forbidded.
In the sanctuary, the priestess would lie down and sleep before the sacred tree(s) of the goddess to recieve an oracle in the form of a prophetic dream.
Rite performed by women seeking aid in childbirth, personal fetility, or livestock abundance.

Asase Ya.description: Goddess of the dead and truth.
fact: Sacrifices are given for favour and blessings.
Two different personalities, but are both one deity: an old woman and a youthful, incredibly beautiful woman.
